Eval workspace: /Users/edeno/Documents/GitHub/spyglass-skill/skills/spyglass-workspace/iteration-3/eval-070-workflow-next-after-dlcmodeltraining/with_skill/

Read eval_metadata.json (the "prompt" field is the user's question). Answer it as a Claude Code session that has triggered the spyglass skill: read /Users/edeno/Documents/GitHub/spyglass-skill/skills/spyglass/SKILL.md and apply its routing/guidance, read referenced files under skills/spyglass/references/ as needed, use bundled scripts at skills/spyglass/scripts/ (code_graph.py, db_graph.py — invoke as `python <full-path> ...`) when they help, and verify against the Spyglass source at /Users/edeno/Documents/GitHub/spyglass/src/spyglass/. Write your full user-facing reply to outputs/response.md inside that directory. Don't write other files; don't ask follow-up questions.

content.text: I'll start by reading the eval metadata and the SKILL.md to understand the task and routing guidance.

content.content:
1	{
2	  "eval_id": 70,
3	  "eval_name": "workflow-next-after-dlcmodeltraining",
4	  "stage": "pipeline-usage",
5	  "tier": "workflow-position",
6	  "difficulty": "hard",
7	  "prompt": "DLC model is trained and in the DLC model table. I want to get from here to a merge_id in `PositionOutput`. What's the ordered list of tables I still need to touch?",
8	  "assertions": {
9	    "required_substrings": [
10	      "DLCPoseEstimation",
11	      "DLCSmoothInterpCohort",
12	      "DLCPosV1",
13	      "PositionOutput.DLCPosV1",
14	      "BodyPart",
15	      "DLCCentroid",
16	      "DLCCentroidSelection",
17	      "DLCOrientation",
18	      "DLCOrientationSelection",
19	      "DLCPosSelection",
20	      "DLCPoseEstimationSelection",
21	      "DLCSmoothInterpCohortSelection",
22	      "DLCSmoothInterpSelection"
23	    ],
24	    "forbidden_substrings": [],
25	    "behavioral_checks": [
26	      "Lists the ordered tables (at least: DLCPoseEstimation → DLCSmoothInterp per bodypart → DLCSmoothInterpCohort → DLCCentroid + DLCOrientation → DLCPosV1 → PositionOutput.DLCPosV1)",
27	      "Mentions the bodypart-aggregation step (DLCSmoothInterpCohort) explicitly — it is the non-obvious step that's easy to skip"
28	    ],
29	    "expected_output_tables_exempt": [
30	      "DLCCentroidParams",
31	      "DLCOrientationParams",
32	      "DLCSmoothInterpParams"
33	    ]
34	  },
35	  "expected_output": "Routes to position_dlc_v1_pipeline.md. Lists the ordered 7-step DLC inference workflow: (1) DLCPoseEstimationSelection.insert_estimation_task → DLCPoseEstimation.populate (writes DLCPoseEstimation.BodyPart per bodypart); (2) DLCSmoothInterpSelection insert (per bodypart) → DLCSmoothInterp.populate; (3) DLCSmoothInterpCohortSelection insert (combines bodyparts) → DLCSmoothInterpCohort.populate; (4) DLCCentroidSelection + DLCCentroid.populate; (5) DLCOrientationSelection + DLCOrientation.populate; (6) DLCPosSelection insert → DLCPosV1.populate. The cohort-projection footgun (the same `dlc_si_cohort_selection_name` is renamed to two aliases — `dlc_si_cohort_centroid` and `dlc_si_cohort_orientation` — by `DLCPosSelection.definition` at `src/spyglass/position/v1/position_dlc_selection.py:33-34`) lives structurally on `DLCPosSelection`, not in `DLCPosV1.populate`; it just surfaces when DLCPosV1 inserts; (7) PositionOutput.DLCPosV1 insert (the merge insert). Notes the cohort step is non-obvious (it aggregates per-bodypart smoothed traces). Each of the per-stage selection tables FKs to its own parameters table — `DLCSmoothInterpSelection -> DLCSmoothInterpParams`, `DLCCentroidSelection -> DLCCentroidParams`, `DLCOrientationSelection -> DLCOrientationParams` — so a row in each params table must exist before the corresponding selection insert. These typically already exist via the labs' `insert_default()` calls; a thorough answer either confirms the defaults are present (`(DLCSmoothInterpParams & {'dlc_si_params_name': 'default'}).fetch1(...)`) or inserts custom rows before the selection step."
36	}
37

1	---
2	name: spyglass
3	description: Use when the task involves Spyglass — the LorenFrankLab
4	  neurophysiology framework built on DataJoint + NWB. Covers setup, NWB
5	  ingestion, pipelines (spike sorting, LFP, ripple, decoding, position,
6	  linearization, DLC), merge tables, paper exports (DANDI/FigURL/Kachery),
7	  custom pipelines, and debugging populate/make/fetch1 failures. Activate
8	  when the task touches any of `import spyglass` / `from spyglass.*`,
9	  `SPYGLASS_BASE_DIR`, `SpyglassMixin`, `merge_get_part`, `merge_restrict`,
10	  pipeline classes (`LFPV1`, `TrodesPosV1`, `DLCPosV1`, `RippleTimesV1`,
11	  `SpikeSorting`, `CurationV1`, `ClusterlessDecodingV1`,
12	  `SortedSpikesDecodingV1`), or DLC/DANDI/Kachery within a Spyglass context
13	  — even if the user doesn't name "Spyglass" explicitly. Do NOT activate
14	  for plain DataJoint without Spyglass imports, unrelated NWB tooling
15	  (pynwb, ndx-*) outside Spyglass, or generic Python/NumPy/pandas debugging
16	  when no Spyglass table is in the call chain.
17	allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash
18	---
19	
20	# Spyglass Data Analysis Skill
21	
22	Router + guardrails for Spyglass work. Pick the right reference from the table below; each reference has the details.
23	
24	## Core Directives
25	
26	- **NEVER delete or drop without explicit confirmation.** The database holds irreplaceable neuroscience data. Any destructive helper (`delete`, `drop`, `cleanup`, `merge_delete`, etc.) must be paired with an inspect step and user confirmation first. `.delete()` on SpyglassMixin tables aliases to `cautious_delete` — it enforces team-based permissions so you can't accidentally delete another lab member's sessions. Paired shapes + protection model: [destructive_operations.md](references/destructive_operations.md).
27	- **Do not invent identifiers.** Verify plausible method, kwarg, field, table, and key names before asserting them. Use the Evidence Expectations below; if unavailable, flag as unconfirmed. Examples: [common_mistakes.md](references/common_mistakes.md).
28	- **Treat pipeline version as load-bearing.** If the user names a versioned class/table, import/path, traceback, or version directory (`CurationV1`, `v1 SortGroup`, `spyglass.spikesorting.v1`, `<pipeline>/<version>/`), verify that version's source before naming classes, methods, kwargs, signatures, tiers, definitions, or workflow steps. Do not infer symmetry; for comparisons, use [feedback_loops.md § Verify behavior, trust identity](references/feedback_loops.md#verify-behavior-trust-identity). If unverified, abstain or flag uncertainty.
29	- **Writes are normal workflow.** Pipelines depend on selection inserts and `populate()` — show the full flow; don't refuse or hedge on the writes.
30	- **Verify cardinality before `fetch1()`, `merge_get_part()`, or `fetch1_dataframe()`** when the restriction is partial. `print(len(rel))`; if >1, `rel.fetch(as_dict=True)` to find missing PK fields. `Table.describe()` shows schema, not count. Carveout: a full-PK restriction is unique — `fetch1()` skips the `len()`. See Common Mistake #2.
31	- **Environment**: detect setup; don't assume Jupyter or remote NWB.
32	- **DataJoint config files**: `dj_local_conf.json` / `~/.datajoint_config.json` hold plaintext `database.password`. Don't `Read`/`cat`; run `python skills/spyglass/scripts/scrub_dj_config.py` (masks secret leaves). Details: [setup_config.md](references/setup_config.md).
33	- **Source of truth**: when the skill and repo disagree, trust the repo. Cite `src/spyglass/...` (drop `src/` for pip installs; locate with `python -c "import spyglass, os; print(os.path.dirname(spyglass.__file__))"`). Tutorials drift — cite `.ipynb`, not `py_scripts/`; when a cell fails on a missing parameter/table/column, check source.
34	- **Do not edit the installed Spyglass package.** Edits to `src/spyglass/...` desync the in-DB schema from what other labs run, and `pip install -e .` silently reverts them. Push back if the user insists.
35	
36	## Evidence Expectations
37	
38	Treat table, key, attribute, method, dependency, parameter, and row-state claims as evidence-backed. Static/source facts: `code_graph.py`, source, or `inspect.signature`. Runtime facts: `db_graph.py`, `Table.heading`, counts, or fetches. Blob keys need source `make()`/builders, docs, or rows. If unavailable, abstain or flag uncertainty.
39	
40	`code_graph.py`/`db_graph.py` means `python skills/spyglass/scripts/<name>`.
41	
42	## Common Mistakes
43	
44	Top 6 highest-frequency bugs. Flag any of these shapes before answering. Expanded prose + three additional footguns: [common_mistakes.md](references/common_mistakes.md).
45	
46	1. **Classmethod restriction discard on merge tables** — `(PositionOutput & merge_key).merge_delete()` drops the `& merge_key`; use `PositionOutput.merge_delete(merge_key)`. Affected methods: [merge_methods.md](references/merge_methods.md).
47	2. **Too-loose restriction + `fetch1()`** — `{"nwb_file_name": f}` matches many rows; add PK fields until `len(rel) == 1`. [datajoint_api.md](references/datajoint_api.md).
48	3. **`skip_duplicates=True` on `insert_sessions`** — raises `TypeError`; use `reinsert=True` for re-ingestion. [ingestion.md](references/ingestion.md).
49	4. **`fetch_nwb()` silently returns a list** on multiple matches (unlike `fetch1()`) — restrict to one row before `[0]`-indexing.
50	5. **Bypassing `cautious_delete` to silence a `PermissionError`** — `.delete()` is team-gated; the error means another lab member owns the session. Coordinate with them, don't reach for `super_delete()` or `force_permission=True`. Protection model + inspect-before-destroy: [destructive_operations.md](references/destructive_operations.md).
51	6. **Silent no-op on merge masters** — `len(MergeMaster & {'nwb_file_name': f})` returns the *whole* table; the master's heading has only `merge_id`, so DataJoint drops the unknown attr. Use `merge_restrict({...})` or `merge_get_part({...})` instead. [merge_methods.md § Silent wrong-count footgun](references/merge_methods.md#silent-wrong-count-footgun).
52	
53	## Feedback Loops
54	
55	Quality-critical ops use validator → fix → proceed. Four loops: post-ingestion verification, pre-`fetch1` cardinality, post-`populate` verification, inspect-before-destroy. Full patterns: [feedback_loops.md](references/feedback_loops.md).
56	
57	## Classify the User's Stage
58	
59	Stages orient vague questions; the Reference Routing table resolves clear topics.
60	
61	1. **Setup/install** → `scripts/install.py` is the canonical fast path per `QUICKSTART.md`. Route to [setup_install.md](references/setup_install.md), [setup_config.md](references/setup_config.md), or [setup_troubleshooting.md](references/setup_troubleshooting.md). `00_Setup.ipynb` is a manual fallback.
62	2. **NWB ingestion** (first data load) → [ingestion.md](references/ingestion.md) + `02_Insert_Data.ipynb`.
63	3. **Framework concepts** (first time using Spyglass) → [merge_methods.md](references/merge_methods.md), [spyglassmixin_methods.md](references/spyglassmixin_methods.md), `01_Concepts.ipynb`.
64	4. **Pipeline usage** (running or querying existing analyses) → pipeline reference files in the table below.
65	5. **Pipeline authoring** (extending a pipeline, writing schema modules) → [custom_pipeline_authoring.md](references/custom_pipeline_authoring.md). Different from usage.
66	6. **Runtime debugging / traceback triage** (populate/make/fetch1 failures, join multiplicity, one-key-fails, NumPy/pandas bugs inside `make()`) → [runtime_debugging.md](references/runtime_debugging.md). Install/config/connection errors go to [setup_troubleshooting.md](references/setup_troubleshooting.md) instead.
67	
68	Users may span stages. Infer from imports/table names; ask only when the answer changes materially or the next step is destructive.
69	
70	## Merge Tables
71	
72	**Decision rule for the 5 merge masters** (`SpikeSortingOutput`, `LFPOutput`, `PositionOutput`, `LinearizedPositionOutput`, `DecodingOutput` — tables with `merge_id` as their only PK field): (1) `& {"nwb_file_name": f}` silently returns the whole table — use `merge_restrict` or `merge_get_part` instead. (2) Load via `merge_get_part(key).fetch1('KEY')` → `(Master & merge_key).fetch1_dataframe()`. (3) `get_restricted_merge_ids` is `SpikeSortingOutput`-only; `fetch_results` is `DecodingOutput`-only. Registry + worked-example: [merge_methods.md](references/merge_methods.md).
73	
74	## Querying an Already-Configured DB
75	
76	If the user hasn't installed or configured Spyglass yet, route to [setup_install.md](references/setup_install.md). For a working install:
77	
78	```python
79	from spyglass.common import Session, IntervalList
80	
81	Session.fetch(limit=10)                      # discover an nwb_file_name
82	IntervalList & {"nwb_file_name": nwb_file}   # discover intervals for it
83	```
84	
85	From here, open the relevant pipeline reference — each starts with a Canonical Example. Don't expand the full workflow inline.
86	
87	## Reference Routing
88	
89	**Load one reference at a time.** Pick the most relevant row; open a second only if needed. This table routes by topic; repo paths live in each reference file.
90	
91	| User question is about... | Load this reference | Canonical notebook |
92	| ------------------------- | ------------------- | ------------------ |
93	| Installing Spyglass | [setup_install.md](references/setup_install.md) | `QUICKSTART.md` + `scripts/install.py`; `00_Setup.ipynb` fallback |
94	| Configuring the database / directories / env vars | [setup_config.md](references/setup_config.md) | `00_Setup.ipynb` |
95	| Setup errors and troubleshooting | [setup_troubleshooting.md](references/setup_troubleshooting.md) | — |
96	| Runtime debugging — populate/make failures, fetch1 cardinality, join multiplicity, one-key-fails | [runtime_debugging.md](references/runtime_debugging.md) | — |
97	| `populate_all_common` silently skipped tables | [populate_all_common_debugging.md](references/populate_all_common_debugging.md) | — |
98	| Destructive operations — deletes, cleanup, inspect-before-destroy | [destructive_operations.md](references/destructive_operations.md) | — |
99	| Validator→fix→proceed loops — post-ingest, pre-fetch1, post-populate, inspect-before-destroy | [feedback_loops.md](references/feedback_loops.md) | — |
100	| Source-graph questions — FK chain A→B, what X declares, owner of method Y, up/downstream | [feedback_loops.md](references/feedback_loops.md) "Three graphs..." → `code_graph.py` | — |
101	| Runtime / DB-graph questions — row existence, counts, merge IDs, set ops, runtime heading vs source heading, source/runtime disagreement, custom tables outside `$SPYGLASS_SRC` | [feedback_loops.md](references/feedback_loops.md) "Three graphs..." → `db_graph.py` | — |
102	| Common Spyglass footguns | [common_mistakes.md](references/common_mistakes.md) | — |
103	| Merge tables (`_Merge` methods) or SpyglassMixin helpers (`fetch_nwb`, `cautious_delete`, `<<`/`>>`) | [merge_methods.md](references/merge_methods.md), [spyglassmixin_methods.md](references/spyglassmixin_methods.md) | `01_Concepts.ipynb`, `04_Merge_Tables.ipynb` |
104	| Group tables (`*Group`, `create_group()`) | [group_tables.md](references/group_tables.md) | — |
105	| NWB ingestion / insert_sessions | [ingestion.md](references/ingestion.md) | `02_Insert_Data.ipynb` |
106	| DataJoint query syntax | [datajoint_api.md](references/datajoint_api.md) | — |
107	| Session, IntervalList, Electrode tables | [common_tables.md](references/common_tables.md) | — |
108	| Spike sorting pipeline (current / v1) | [spikesorting_v1_pipeline.md](references/spikesorting_v1_pipeline.md) | `10_Spike_SortingV1.ipynb` |
109	| Spike sorting analysis (post-pipeline: `SortedSpikesGroup`, `UnitAnnotation`, spike-time/firing-rate helpers) | [spikesorting_v1_analysis.md](references/spikesorting_v1_analysis.md) | `11_Spike_Sorting_Analysis.ipynb` |
110	| Reading v0 legacy code / v0 data | [spikesorting_v0_legacy.md](references/spikesorting_v0_legacy.md) | `10_Spike_SortingV0.ipynb` |
111	| Position tracking — overview / merge layer / imported pose | [position_pipeline.md](references/position_pipeline.md) | — |
112	| Position tracking — Trodes (LED) | [position_trodes_v1_pipeline.md](references/position_trodes_v1_pipeline.md) | `20_Position_Trodes.ipynb` |
113	| Position tracking — DeepLabCut | [position_dlc_v1_pipeline.md](references/position_dlc_v1_pipeline.md) | `21_DLC.ipynb` |
114	| Linearization | [linearization_pipeline.md](references/linearization_pipeline.md) | `24_Linearization.ipynb` |
115	| LFP / theta | [lfp_pipeline.md](references/lfp_pipeline.md) | `30_LFP.ipynb`, `31_Theta.ipynb` |
116	| Ripple detection | [ripple_pipeline.md](references/ripple_pipeline.md) | `32_Ripple_Detection.ipynb` |
117	| Decoding (clusterless / sorted) | [decoding_pipeline.md](references/decoding_pipeline.md) | `40_Extracting_Clusterless_Waveform_Features.ipynb`, `41_Decoding_Clusterless.ipynb`, `42_Decoding_SortedSpikes.ipynb` |
118	| MUA detection | [mua_pipeline.md](references/mua_pipeline.md) | `50_MUA_Detection.ipynb` |
119	| Behavior / MoSeq | [behavior_pipeline.md](references/behavior_pipeline.md) | `60_MoSeq.ipynb` |
120	| Cross-table exploration | [workflows.md](references/workflows.md) | — |
121	| Export for papers / reproducible snapshots | [export.md](references/export.md) | `05_Export.ipynb` |
122	| Syncing / sharing with collaborators (Kachery) | [setup_config.md](references/setup_config.md) — "Data Sharing Tables (Kachery)" | `03_Data_Sync.ipynb` |

content.text: The skill router points to `position_dlc_v1_pipeline.md`. Let me read that reference, plus check the source for the cohort-projection footgun cited in expected output.

content.content:
1	# Position Pipeline — DeepLabCut (DLC v1)
2	
3	Multi-stage video-based pose estimation. The DLC pipeline lives
4	under `spyglass.position.v1`; the populate handler for `DLCPosV1.make`
5	auto-inserts the resulting row into `PositionOutput` via
6	`PositionOutput._merge_insert(...)` (`position/v1/position_dlc_selection.py:85`).
7	
8	For the umbrella merge layer (`PositionOutput`, the per-source
9	method matrix, and the imported-pose path), see
10	[position_pipeline.md](position_pipeline.md). For Trodes LED
11	tracking, see [position_trodes_v1_pipeline.md](position_trodes_v1_pipeline.md).
12	
13	## Contents
14	
15	- [Pipeline Flow](#pipeline-flow)
16	- [Key DLC Tables](#key-dlc-tables)
17	- [Key DLC Invariants](#key-dlc-invariants)
18	- [Gotcha — pose estimation hangs on existing video](#gotcha--pose-estimation-hangs-on-existing-video)
19	- [Gotcha — empty PositionIntervalMap](#gotcha--empty-positionintervalmap-on-old-ingestions-or-dlc-only-sessions)
20	- [Gotcha — DLC env vars must be set in the kernel](#gotcha--dlc-env-vars-must-be-set-in-the-kernel)
21	- [Gotcha — `test_mode` string vs bool](#dlc-test_mode-string-vs-bool-gotcha)
22	- [DLC Parameter Tables](#dlc-parameter-tables)
23	- [DLC Data Access](#dlc-data-access)
24	
25	## Pipeline Flow
26	
27	```text
28	DLCProject → DLCModelSource → DLCModelSelection → DLCModel
29	                                                       ↓
30	DLCPoseEstimationSelection → DLCPoseEstimation → DLCPoseEstimation.BodyPart
31	                                                       ↓
32	DLCSmoothInterpParams → DLCSmoothInterpSelection → DLCSmoothInterp (per bodypart)
33	                                                       ↓
34	DLCSmoothInterpCohortSelection → DLCSmoothInterpCohort (combines bodyparts)
35	        ↓                                    ↓
36	DLCCentroidParams → DLCCentroidSelection → DLCCentroid
37	DLCOrientationParams → DLCOrientationSelection → DLCOrientation
38	        ↓                                    ↓
39	                    DLCPosSelection → DLCPosV1 → PositionOutput.DLCPosV1
40	```
41	
42	## Key DLC Tables
43	
44	```python
45	from spyglass.position.v1 import (
46	    BodyPart, DLCProject,
47	    DLCModel, DLCModelParams, DLCModelSelection, DLCModelSource,
48	    DLCPoseEstimation, DLCPoseEstimationSelection,
49	    DLCSmoothInterp, DLCSmoothInterpParams, DLCSmoothInterpSelection,
50	    DLCSmoothInterpCohort, DLCSmoothInterpCohortSelection,
51	    DLCCentroid, DLCCentroidParams, DLCCentroidSelection,
52	    DLCOrientation, DLCOrientationParams, DLCOrientationSelection,
53	    DLCPosSelection, DLCPosV1,
54	)
55	```
56	
57	(All four `*Selection` classes are exported from
58	`spyglass.position.v1`'s `__init__.py` — they appear in the pipeline
59	flow above and are required by their respective `populate` calls.)
60	
61	## Key DLC Invariants
62	
63	For the full 7-step DLC inference workflow (`insert_estimation_task` → `DLCPoseEstimation` → `DLCSmoothInterp` per-bodypart → `DLCSmoothInterpCohort` → `DLCCentroid` + `DLCOrientation` → `DLCPosV1` → `PositionOutput`), see `21_DLC.ipynb`. Two things the notebook does not flag that will bite you:
64	
65	- Each selection stage shares the `DLCPoseEstimation` foreign key but adds its own parameter name; build the key by extending `pose_estimation_key` one field at a time, not by reusing the previous stage's full key.
66	- **`DLCPosSelection` is the exception to that pattern.** It projects `dlc_si_cohort_selection_name` into two separate aliases (`dlc_si_cohort_centroid` and `dlc_si_cohort_orientation`), so those must be set explicitly — spreading `cohort_key` leaves the wrong field names and `populate()` silently produces no rows.
67	
68	## Gotcha — pose estimation hangs on existing video
69	
70	`DLCPoseEstimationSelection.insert_estimation_task(...)` never completes (no error, no progress) when an `.mp4` with the same name already sits in the DLC target video directory. The path is whatever the install sets for `dlc_video_dir` (check `spyglass.settings` or `dj.config['custom']['dlc_dirs']`; this is a site/lab-specific path, not a Spyglass-shipped default). Manually delete the stale `.mp4` before re-running. If the call appears to hang, this is the first thing to check.
71	
72	## Gotcha — empty PositionIntervalMap on old ingestions or DLC-only sessions
73	
74	If DLC populate crashes with `IndexError: index 0 is out of bounds for axis 0 with size 0` from `convert_epoch_interval_name_to_position_interval_name`, the session has no resolvable `PositionIntervalMap` row. Note that `DLCPoseEstimation`'s `make()` already calls `convert_epoch_interval_name_to_position_interval_name` internally with `populate_missing=True` (`position/v1/position_dlc_pose_estimation.py:255-263`), so re-running the converter manually rarely helps — the converter ran, didn't resolve, and `DLCPoseEstimation` continued by falling back to video timestamps (see DLC-only below). The IndexError comes from a *downstream* step (typically `DLCCentroid` / `DLCOrientation` selection or the `RawPosition` lookup at `position_dlc_pose_estimation.py:264`) that still expects a real position interval. The diagnostic is upstream:
75	
76	```python
77	from spyglass.common import convert_epoch_interval_name_to_position_interval_name
78	
79	# Check what the converter actually returned for this epoch — None
80	# means it inserted a null map row and there's no position interval
81	# to resolve (`common/common_behav.py:886, 955`).
82	convert_epoch_interval_name_to_position_interval_name(
83	    {'nwb_file_name': nwb_file, 'epoch': epoch_id},
84	    populate_missing=True,
85	)
86	```
87	
88	**DLC-only sessions** (no Trodes-derived position). The converter only accepts interval names that pass `PositionSource._is_valid_name()` (matches `pos N valid times`, `common/common_behav.py:955`); when none are present it inserts a *null* map row rather than raising (`common/common_behav.py:886`). Two paths exist:
89	
90	- **Raw position is available for the session.** Populate `PositionSource` / `RawPosition` first so the `pos N valid times` intervals exist, then the mapper can resolve them.
91	- **Genuinely DLC-only.** `DLCPoseEstimation.make()` already falls back to the source video's timestamps when the mapper returns no position-interval name (`position/v1/position_dlc_pose_estimation.py:264`) — you don't need to invent a `RawPosition` row to make pose estimation run. Failures further downstream (`DLCCentroid`, `DLCOrientation`) only appear if those selections were inserted and *those* steps require a position interval; configure the DLC pipeline to stop after `DLCPoseEstimation` if your session has no Trodes-side position.
92	
93	## Gotcha — DLC env vars must be set in the kernel
94	
95	IDEs / SSH sessions frequently don't source the login profile, so `os.environ['DLC_OUTPUT_DIR']` returns `None` in the Python kernel and paths like `None/<video>.mp4` or `Path(None)` cause `TypeError` / `ffprobe` errors deep in the populate.
96	
97	**Check.** The canonical names current Spyglass reads are `DLC_PROJECT_DIR`, `DLC_VIDEO_DIR`, `DLC_OUTPUT_DIR` — `settings.py` derives them as `f"{dir_type.upper()}_{dir.upper()}_DIR"` via `dir_to_var()` (see `settings.py:335`). `DLC_PROJECT_PATH` is only a legacy / base-dir fallback (`settings.py:207`); newer code paths expect `_DIR`.
98	
99	```python
100	import os
101	need = ['DLC_PROJECT_DIR', 'DLC_VIDEO_DIR', 'DLC_OUTPUT_DIR',
102	        'HDF5_USE_FILE_LOCKING']
103	missing = [k for k in need if not os.environ.get(k)]
104	if missing:
105	    raise RuntimeError(f'DLC env vars not set: {missing}')
106	```
107	
108	**Fix.** Prefer routing DLC paths through `dj.config['custom']['dlc_dirs']` (see `setup_config.md` "Per-Directory Overrides") and `dj.config.save_global()` — `dj.config` is honored from any kernel, unlike `~/.bashrc`. `HDF5_USE_FILE_LOCKING='FALSE'` is required on shared filesystems.
109	
110	## DLC: `test_mode` string vs bool gotcha
111	
112	Symptom: `DLCModelTraining.populate(...)` finishes in minutes with no useful model; log says `max_iters=2`.
113	
114	Root cause: `spyglass.settings.test_mode` was saved as the string `'false'` (not the boolean `False`) in the user's `dj.config`. Python evaluates `bool('false')` as `True`, so Spyglass's test-mode code path — which shortens DLC training to 2 iterations — runs in production.
115	
116	**Check.**
117	
118	```python
119	from spyglass.settings import test_mode
120	print(test_mode, type(test_mode))   # must be <class 'bool'>
121	```
122	
123	If the type is `str`, remove the entry from your DataJoint config file (or overwrite with an actual Python `False`) and restart the kernel.
124	
125	**Current Spyglass handles this.** `SpyglassConfig` applies `str_to_bool` on load (`src/spyglass/settings.py:171`), so the type check will always show `bool` on a current install. If you see `type(test_mode) == str` on current Spyglass, something is overwriting `test_mode` after load — investigate the import path.
126	
127	Sanity check if the training keeps stopping early even with a proper bool: look at the DLC project `config.yaml`'s `num_iterations` — DLC itself may have a per-project cap independent of Spyglass.
128	
129	## DLC Parameter Tables
130	
131	| Table | Key |
132	| ------- | ----- |
133	| `DLCModelParams` | `dlc_model_params_name` |
134	| `DLCSmoothInterpParams` | `dlc_si_params_name` |
135	| `DLCCentroidParams` | `dlc_centroid_params_name` |
136	| `DLCOrientationParams` | `dlc_orientation_params_name` |
137	
138	Use `TableName.describe()` to see exact parameter fields for each.
139	
140	**Validating a proposed params dict before insert.** `spyglass.position.v1.dlc_utils` exposes `validate_option`, `validate_list`, and `validate_smooth_params` — the same validators the DLC pipeline uses internally. Call them before inserting a custom Params row to catch bad values at author time instead of `populate()` time. These are also the idiomatic validators to reuse when authoring a new params table for a custom pipeline downstream of DLC.
141	
142	Defaults check:
143	
144	```python
145	DLCSmoothInterpParams.fetch('dlc_si_params_name')
146	```
147	
148	## DLC Data Access
149	
150	```python
151	# Get pose data for all bodyparts
152	pose_df = (PositionOutput & merge_key).fetch_pose_dataframe()
153	
154	# Get raw pose estimation for a bodypart (before smoothing)
155	(DLCPoseEstimation.BodyPart & key).fetch1_dataframe()
156	# Columns: video_frame_ind, x, y, likelihood
157	
158	# Get smoothed pose for a bodypart
159	(DLCSmoothInterp & key).fetch1_dataframe()
160	# Columns: video_frame_ind, x, y
161	```
162	
163	## Fetch via PositionOutput
164	
165	```python
166	# Per-DLC video path (defined at position/v1/position_dlc_selection.py:315):
167	video_path = (PositionOutput & merge_key).fetch_video_path()
168	
169	# Per-DLC position dataframe (same columns as Trodes):
170	position_df = (PositionOutput & merge_key).fetch1_dataframe()
171	```
172

6	import pandas as pd
7	import pynwb

9	
10	from spyglass.common.common_nwbfile import AnalysisNwbfile
11	from spyglass.position.v1.dlc_utils_makevid import make_video
12	from spyglass.position.v1.position_dlc_centroid import DLCCentroid
13	from spyglass.position.v1.position_dlc_cohort import DLCSmoothInterpCohort
14	from spyglass.position.v1.position_dlc_orient import DLCOrientation
15	from spyglass.position.v1.position_dlc_pose_estimation import (
16	    DLCPoseEstimation,

18	)

22	schema = dj.schema("position_v1_dlc_selection")
23	
24	
25	@schema
26	class DLCPosSelection(SpyglassMixin, dj.Manual):
27	    """
28	    Specify collection of upstream DLCCentroid and DLCOrientation entries
29	    to combine into a set of position information
30	    """
31	
32	    definition = """
33	    -> DLCCentroid.proj(dlc_si_cohort_centroid='dlc_si_cohort_selection_name', centroid_analysis_file_name='analysis_file_name')
34	    -> DLCOrientation.proj(dlc_si_cohort_orientation='dlc_si_cohort_selection_name', orientation_analysis_file_name='analysis_file_name')
35	    """
36	
37	
38	@schema
39	class DLCPosV1(SpyglassMixin, dj.Computed):
40	    """
41	    Combines upstream DLCCentroid and DLCOrientation
42	    entries into a single entry with a single Analysis NWB file
43	    """
44	
45	    definition = """
46	    -> DLCPosSelection
47	    ---
48	    -> AnalysisNwbfile
49	    position_object_id      : varchar(80)
50	    orientation_object_id   : varchar(80)
51	    velocity_object_id      : varchar(80)
52	    pose_eval_result        : longblob
53	    """
54	
55	    def make(self, key):
56	        """Populate the table with the combined position information.
57	
58	        1. Fetches position and orientation data from the DLCCentroid and
59	        DLCOrientation tables.
60	        2. Creates NWB objects for position, orientation, and velocity.
61	        3. Generates an AnalysisNwbfile and adds the NWB objects to it.
62	        4. Inserts the key into the table, and the PositionOutput Merge table.
63	        """
64	        orig_key = copy.deepcopy(key)
65	        # Add to Analysis NWB file
66	        key["pose_eval_result"] = self.evaluate_pose_estimation(key)
67	
68	        pos_nwb = (DLCCentroid & key).fetch_nwb()[0]
69	        ori_nwb = (DLCOrientation & key).fetch_nwb()[0]
70	        key = (
71	            self.make_null_position_nwb(key)
72	            if isinstance(pos_nwb["dlc_position"], pd.DataFrame)  # null case
73	            else self.make_dlc_pos_nwb(key, pos_nwb, ori_nwb)  # normal case
74	        )
75	
76	        AnalysisNwbfile().add(
77	            nwb_file_name=key["nwb_file_name"],
78	            analysis_file_name=key["analysis_file_name"],
79	        )
80	        self.insert1(key)
81	
82	        from ..position_merge import PositionOutput
83	
84	        # TODO: The next line belongs in a merge table function
85	        PositionOutput._merge_insert(
86	            [orig_key],
87	            part_name=to_camel_case(self.table_name.split("__")[-1]),
88	            skip_duplicates=True,
89	        )
90	        AnalysisNwbfile().log(key, table=self.full_table_name)
91	
92	    @staticmethod
93	    def make_null_position_nwb(key):
94	        a_fname = AnalysisNwbfile().create(nwb_file_name=key["nwb_file_name"])
95	        obj_id = AnalysisNwbfile().add_nwb_object(a_fname, pd.DataFrame())
96	        return dict(  # modified to avoid editing the original key
97	            key,
98	            analysis_file_name=a_fname,
99	            position_object_id=obj_id,
100	            orientation_object_id=obj_id,
101	            velocity_object_id=obj_id,
102	        )
103	
104	    @staticmethod
105	    def make_dlc_pos_nwb(key, pos_nwb, ori_nwb):
106	        pos_obj = pos_nwb["dlc_position"].spatial_series["position"]
107	        vel_obj = pos_nwb["dlc_velocity"].time_series["velocity"]
108	        vid_frame_obj = pos_nwb["dlc_velocity"].time_series["video_frame_ind"]
109	        ori_obj = ori_nwb["dlc_orientation"].spatial_series["orientation"]
110	
111	        position = pynwb.behavior.Position()
112	        orientation = pynwb.behavior.CompassDirection()
113	        velocity = pynwb.behavior.BehavioralTimeSeries()
114	
115	        position.create_spatial_series(
116	            name=pos_obj.name,
117	            timestamps=np.asarray(pos_obj.timestamps),
118	            conversion=pos_obj.conversion,
119	            data=np.asarray(pos_obj.data),
120	            reference_frame=pos_obj.reference_frame,
121	            comments=pos_obj.comments,
122	            description=pos_obj.description,
123	        )
124	
125	        orientation.create_spatial_series(
126	            name=ori_obj.name,
127	            timestamps=np.asarray(ori_obj.timestamps),
128	            conversion=ori_obj.conversion,
129	            data=np.asarray(ori_obj.data),
130	            reference_frame=ori_obj.reference_frame,
131	            comments=ori_obj.comments,
132	            description=ori_obj.description,
133	        )
134	
135	        velocity.create_timeseries(
136	            name=vel_obj.name,
137	            timestamps=np.asarray(vel_obj.timestamps),
138	            conversion=vel_obj.conversion,
139	            unit=vel_obj.unit,
140	            data=np.asarray(vel_obj.data),
141	            comments=vel_obj.comments,
142	            description=vel_obj.description,
143	        )
144	
145	        velocity.create_timeseries(
146	            name=vid_frame_obj.name,
147	            timestamps=np.asarray(vid_frame_obj.timestamps),
148	            unit=vid_frame_obj.unit,
149	            data=np.asarray(vid_frame_obj.data),
150	            description=vid_frame_obj.description,
151	            comments=vid_frame_obj.comments,
152	        )
153	
154	        # Add to Analysis NWB file
155	        analysis_file_name = AnalysisNwbfile().create(key["nwb_file_name"])
156	        key["analysis_file_name"] = analysis_file_name
157	        nwb_analysis_file = AnalysisNwbfile()
158	
159	        key.update(
160	            {
161	                "analysis_file_name": analysis_file_name,
162	                "position_object_id": nwb_analysis_file.add_nwb_object(
163	                    analysis_file_name, position
164	                ),
165	                "orientation_object_id": nwb_analysis_file.add_nwb_object(
166	                    analysis_file_name, orientation
167	                ),
168	                "velocity_object_id": nwb_analysis_file.add_nwb_object(
169	                    analysis_file_name, velocity
170	                ),
171	            }
172	        )
173	        return key
174	
175	    def fetch1_dataframe(self) -> pd.DataFrame:
176	        """Return the position data as a DataFrame."""
177	        _ = self.ensure_single_entry()
178	        nwb_data = self.fetch_nwb()[0]
179	        index = pd.Index(
180	            np.asarray(nwb_data["position"].get_spatial_series().timestamps),
181	            name="time",
182	        )
183	        COLUMNS = [
184	            "video_frame_ind",
185	            "position_x",
186	            "position_y",
187	            "orientation",
188	            "velocity_x",
189	            "velocity_y",
190	            "speed",
191	        ]
192	        return pd.DataFrame(
193	            np.concatenate(
194	                (
195	                    np.asarray(
196	                        nwb_data["velocity"]
197	                        .time_series["video_frame_ind"]
198	                        .data,
199	                        dtype=int,
200	                    )[:, np.newaxis],
201	                    np.asarray(nwb_data["position"].get_spatial_series().data),
202	                    np.asarray(
203	                        nwb_data["orientation"].get_spatial_series().data
204	                    )[:, np.newaxis],
205	                    np.asarray(
206	                        nwb_data["velocity"].time_series["velocity"].data
207	                    ),
208	                ),
209	                axis=1,
210	            ),
211	            columns=COLUMNS,
212	            index=index,
213	        )
214	
215	    def fetch_nwb(self, **kwargs):
216	        """Fetch the NWB file."""
217	        attrs = [a for a in self.heading.names if not a == "pose_eval_result"]
218	        return super().fetch_nwb(*attrs, **kwargs)
219	
220	    @classmethod
221	    def evaluate_pose_estimation(cls, key):
222	        """Evaluate the pose estimation."""
223	        likelihood_thresh = []
224	
225	        valid_fields = DLCSmoothInterpCohort.BodyPart().heading.names
226	        centroid_key = {k: val for k, val in key.items() if k in valid_fields}
227	        centroid_key["dlc_si_cohort_selection_name"] = key[
228	            "dlc_si_cohort_centroid"
229	        ]
230	        centroid_bodyparts, centroid_si_params = (
231	            DLCSmoothInterpCohort.BodyPart & centroid_key
232	        ).fetch("bodypart", "dlc_si_params_name")
233	
234	        orientation_key = centroid_key.copy()
235	        orientation_key["dlc_si_cohort_selection_name"] = key[
236	            "dlc_si_cohort_orientation"
237	        ]
238	
239	        # check for the null cohort case
240	        if not (DLCSmoothInterpCohort.BodyPart & centroid_key) and not (
241	            DLCSmoothInterpCohort.BodyPart & orientation_key
242	        ):
243	            return {}  # pragma: no cover
244	
245	        centroid_bodyparts, centroid_si_params = (
246	            DLCSmoothInterpCohort.BodyPart & centroid_key
247	        ).fetch("bodypart", "dlc_si_params_name")
248	        orientation_bodyparts, orientation_si_params = (
249	            DLCSmoothInterpCohort.BodyPart & orientation_key
250	        ).fetch("bodypart", "dlc_si_params_name")
251	
252	        for param in np.unique(
253	            np.concatenate((centroid_si_params, orientation_si_params))
254	        ):
255	            likelihood_thresh.append(
256	                (
257	                    DLCSmoothInterpParams() & {"dlc_si_params_name": param}
258	                ).fetch1("params")["likelihood_thresh"]
259	            )
260	
261	        if len(np.unique(likelihood_thresh)) > 1:
262	            raise ValueError(  # pragma: no cover
263	                "more than one likelihood threshold used"
264	            )
265	
266	        like_thresh = likelihood_thresh[0]
267	        bodyparts = np.unique([*centroid_bodyparts, *orientation_bodyparts])
268	        fields = DLCPoseEstimation.BodyPart.heading.names
269	        pose_estimation_key = {k: v for k, v in key.items() if k in fields}
270	        pose_estimation_df = pd.concat(
271	            {
272	                bodypart: (
273	                    DLCPoseEstimation.BodyPart()
274	                    & {**pose_estimation_key, **{"bodypart": bodypart}}
275	                ).fetch1_dataframe()
276	                for bodypart in bodyparts.tolist()
277	            },
278	            axis=1,
279	        )
280	        df_filter = {
281	            bodypart: pose_estimation_df[bodypart]["likelihood"] < like_thresh
282	            for bodypart in bodyparts
283	            if bodypart in pose_estimation_df.columns
284	        }
285	        sub_thresh_percent_dict = {
286	            bodypart: (
287	                len(
288	                    np.where(
289	                        ~np.isnan(
290	                            pose_estimation_df[bodypart]["likelihood"].where(
291	                                df_filter[bodypart]
292	                            )
293	                        )
294	                    )[0]
295	                )
296	                / len(pose_estimation_df)
297	            )
298	            * 100
299	            for bodypart in bodyparts
300	        }
301	        return sub_thresh_percent_dict
302	
303	    def fetch_pose_dataframe(self):
304	        """fetches the pose data from the pose estimation table
305	
306	        Returns
307	        -------
308	        pd.DataFrame
309	            pose data
310	        """
311	        _ = self.ensure_single_entry()
312	        key = self.fetch1("KEY")
313	        return (DLCPoseEstimation & key).fetch_dataframe()
314	
315	    def fetch_video_path(self, key: dict = dict()) -> str:
316	        """Return the video path for pose estimate
317	
318	        Parameters
319	        ----------
320	        key : dict, optional
321	            key of entry within the table instance, by default dict()
322	        Returns
323	        -------
324	        str
325	            absolute path to video file
326	        """
327	        key = (self & key).fetch1("KEY")
328	        return (DLCPoseEstimationSelection & key).fetch1("video_path")
329	
330	
331	@schema
332	class DLCPosVideoParams(SpyglassMixin, dj.Manual):
333	    """Parameters for the video generation.
334	
335	    Attributes
336	    ----------
337	    dlc_pos_video_params_name : str
338	        Name of the parameter set.
339	    params : dict
340	        Parameters for the video generation, including...
341	        percent_frames : int
342	            Percentage of frames to include in the video.
343	        incl_likelihood : bool
344	            Whether to include likelihood in the video.
345	        video_params : dict, optional
346	            additional parameters passed to VideoMaker, like arrow_radius,
347	            circle_radius
348	    """
349	
350	    definition = """
351	    dlc_pos_video_params_name : varchar(50)
352	    ---
353	    params : blob
354	    """
355	
356	    @classmethod
357	    def insert_default(cls):
358	        """Insert the default parameters."""
359	        params = {
360	            "percent_frames": 1,
361	            "incl_likelihood": True,
362	            "video_params": {
363	                "arrow_radius": 20,
364	                "circle_radius": 6,
365	            },
366	        }
367	        cls.insert1(
368	            {"dlc_pos_video_params_name": "default", "params": params},
369	            skip_duplicates=True,
370	        )
371	
372	    @classmethod
373	    def get_default(cls):
374	        """Return the default parameters."""
375	        query = cls & {"dlc_pos_video_params_name": "default"}
376	        if not len(query) > 0:
377	            cls().insert_default()
378	            default = (cls & {"dlc_pos_video_params_name": "default"}).fetch1()
379	        else:
380	            default = query.fetch1()
381	        return default
382	
383	
384	@schema
385	class DLCPosVideoSelection(SpyglassMixin, dj.Manual):
386	    definition = """
387	    -> DLCPosV1
388	    -> DLCPosVideoParams
389	    ---
390	    """
391	
392	
393	@schema
394	class DLCPosVideo(SpyglassMixin, dj.Computed):
395	    """Creates a video of the computed head position and orientation as well as
396	    the original LED positions overlaid on the video of the animal.
397	
398	    Use for debugging the effect of position extraction parameters."""
399	
400	    definition = """
401	    -> DLCPosVideoSelection
402	    ---
403	    """
404	
405	    def make(self, key):
406	        """Populate the DLCPosVideo table.
407	
408	        1. Fetches parameters from the DLCPosVideoParams table.
409	        2. Fetches position interval name from epoch name.
410	        3. Fetches pose estimation data and video information.
411	        4. Fetches centroid and likelihood data for each bodypart.
412	        5. Calls make_video to create the video with the above data.
413	        """
414	        M_TO_CM = 100
415	
416	        params = (DLCPosVideoParams & key).fetch1("params")
417	        epoch = key["epoch"]
418	
419	        pose_est_key = {
420	            "nwb_file_name": key["nwb_file_name"],
421	            "epoch": epoch,
422	            "dlc_model_name": key["dlc_model_name"],
423	            "dlc_model_params_name": key["dlc_model_params_name"],
424	        }
425	
426	        pose_estimation_params, video_filename, output_dir, meters_per_pixel = (
427	            DLCPoseEstimationSelection * DLCPoseEstimation & pose_est_key
428	        ).fetch1(
429	            "pose_estimation_params",
430	            "video_path",
431	            "pose_estimation_output_dir",
432	            "meters_per_pixel",
433	        )
434	        if pose_estimation_params is None:
435	            pose_estimation_params = dict()
436	
437	        self._info_msg(f"video filename: {video_filename}")
438	        self._info_msg("Loading position data...")
439	
440	        v1_key = {k: v for k, v in key.items() if k in DLCPosV1.primary_key}
441	        pos_info_df = (
442	            DLCPosV1() & {"epoch": epoch, **v1_key}
443	        ).fetch1_dataframe()
444	        pos_est_df = pd.concat(
445	            {
446	                bodypart: (
447	                    DLCPoseEstimation.BodyPart()
448	                    & {**pose_est_key, **{"bodypart": bodypart}}
449	                ).fetch1_dataframe()
450	                for bodypart in (DLCSmoothInterpCohort.BodyPart & pose_est_key)
451	                .fetch("bodypart")
452	                .tolist()
453	            },
454	            axis=1,
455	        )
456	        if not len(pos_est_df) == len(pos_info_df):
457	            raise ValueError(  # pragma: no cover
458	                "Dataframes are not the same length\n"
459	                + f"\tPose estim   :  {len(pos_est_df)}\n"
460	                + f"\tPosition info: {len(pos_info_df)}"
461	            )
462	
463	        output_video_filename = (
464	            key["nwb_file_name"].replace(".nwb", "")
465	            + f"_{epoch:02d}_"
466	            + f'{key["dlc_si_cohort_centroid"]}_'
467	            + f'{key["dlc_centroid_params_name"]}'
468	            + f'{key["dlc_orientation_params_name"]}.mp4'
469	        )
470	        if Path(output_dir).exists():
471	            output_video_filename = Path(output_dir) / output_video_filename
472	
473	        idx = pd.IndexSlice
474	        video_frame_inds = pos_info_df["video_frame_ind"].astype(int).to_numpy()
475	        centroids = {
476	            bodypart: pos_est_df.loc[:, idx[bodypart, ("x", "y")]].to_numpy()
477	            for bodypart in pos_est_df.columns.levels[0]
478	        }
479	        likelihoods = (
480	            {
481	                bodypart: pos_est_df.loc[
482	                    :, idx[bodypart, ("likelihood")]
483	                ].to_numpy()
484	                for bodypart in pos_est_df.columns.levels[0]
485	            }
486	            if params.get("incl_likelihood")
487	            else None
488	        )
489	        frames = params.get("frames", None)
490	        percent_frames = params.get("percent_frames", None)
491	
492	        if limit := params.get("limit", None):  # new int param for debugging
493	            output_video_filename = Path(".") / f"TEST_VID_{limit}.mp4"
494	            video_frame_inds = video_frame_inds[:limit]
495	            percent_frames = 1
496	            pos_info_df = pos_info_df.head(limit)
497	
498	        video_maker = make_video(
499	            video_filename=video_filename,
500	            video_frame_inds=video_frame_inds,
501	            position_mean={
502	                "DLC": np.asarray(pos_info_df[["position_x", "position_y"]])
503	            },
504	            orientation_mean={"DLC": np.asarray(pos_info_df[["orientation"]])},
505	            centroids=centroids,
506	            likelihoods=likelihoods,
507	            position_time=np.asarray(pos_info_df.index),
508	            processor=params.get("processor", "matplotlib"),
509	            frames=np.arange(frames[0], frames[1]) if frames else None,
510	            percent_frames=percent_frames,
511	            output_video_filename=output_video_filename,
512	            cm_to_pixels=meters_per_pixel * M_TO_CM,
513	            crop=pose_estimation_params.get("cropping"),
514	            key_hash=dj.hash.key_hash(key),
515	            debug=params.get("debug", False),
516	            **params.get("video_params", {}),
517	        )
518	
519	        if output_video_filename.exists():
520	            self.insert1(key)
521	
522	        if limit:
523	            return video_maker
524
